title: Room-temperature intrinsic ferromagnetism in epitaxial CrTe2 ultrathin films

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports epitaxial growth of stoichiometric mono- to few-layer CrTe2 on bilayer graphene/SiC by MBE and demonstrates intrinsic ferromagnetism persisting to room temperature, with strong perpendicular magnetic anisotropy and a spin-split metallic band structure. The magnetic order is supported by SQUID, XMCD, and ARPES, and the monolayer still shows ferromagnetism with TC around 200 K, making ultrathin CrTe2 a promising 2D magnet for spintronics.

figures.description: Schematic of MBE growth of CrTe2 on graphene/SiC.
STM topography of a 7 ML CrTe2 film showing atomically flat terraces; inset optical image of a large-area film.
Line-scan profile across STM terraces showing ~6.14 Å step height.
XRD 2θ-ω scan with Laue fringes around CrTe2 (001) reflections and fitted thickness/roughness/c-lattice constant.
Atomically resolved STM image showing hexagonal lattice.
Line profile from atomically resolved STM image giving ~3.81 Å lattice periodicity.
Temperature-dependent SQUID magnetization curves for CrTe2 films of different thicknesses under out-of-plane field.
Out-of-plane M-H hysteresis loops of 7 ML CrTe2 at different temperatures.
In-plane M-H hysteresis loops of 7 ML CrTe2 at different temperatures.
Expanded hysteresis loops at 300 K and inset temperature dependence of Ku for 7 ML CrTe2.
figures.data_type: magnetization hysteresis curve / derived anisotropy plot

figures.description: Schematic of XMCD measurement geometry.
XAS and XMCD spectra of 7 ML CrTe2 from 5 K to 300 K, including integrals at 5 K.
Enlarged left- and right-circularly polarized XAS near the Cr L3 edge at 200 K, 250 K, and 300 K.
Spin and orbital magnetic moments versus temperature derived from XMCD sum-rule analysis.
XAS and XMCD spectra of 1 ML CrTe2 at various temperatures.
Enlarged XAS near the Cr L3 edge for 1 ML CrTe2 showing helicity-dependent difference.
XMCD percentage versus temperature for 1 ML CrTe2.
Thickness-temperature phase diagram compiling TC from XMCD and SQUID.
ARPES valence-band dispersion of 7 ML CrTe2 along M-Γ-K.
DFT-calculated spin-polarized band structure of 7 ML CrTe2.
ARPES near-EF dispersion measured with He Iα photons and MDC peak fits.
ARPES near-EF dispersion measured with He IIα photons and MDC peak fits.
Combined experimental and theoretical near-EF band structure comparison.
Thickness-dependent ARPES intensity maps from 1 ML to 15 ML.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include in-situ STM topography images, XRD scans, TEM/HAADF-STEM images, SQUID magnetization data, XAS/XMCD spectra at Cr L2,3 edges, and ARPES spectra collected with He Iα and He IIα photons. The paper also mentions in-situ transfer under UHV and repeated measurements across temperatures and thicknesses.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Processed/derived plots include STM line profiles, fitted XRD thickness/roughness/lattice constant, SQUID M-T and M-H curves, extracted anisotropy Ku, XMCD-derived spin and orbital moments using sum rules, XMCD percentage versus temperature, thickness-dependent TC phase diagram, MDC peak-traced ARPES dispersions, and experimental-theory band comparisons.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The authors state that the main data supporting the findings are available within the article and its Supplementary Information files, and that extra data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The paper describes raw data as instrument measurements from STM, XRD, TEM, SQUID, XAS/XMCD, and ARPES, but does not specify deposited raw file formats, repositories, or file organization. Measurements were collected in UHV and at beamlines/instruments, with details given in Methods.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include fitted profiles, extracted lattice constants and thicknesses from XRD, magnetization curves and hysteresis loops, derived Ku values, XMCD sum-rule-derived ms and ml, XMCD percentages, TC estimates from fitting, MDC peak positions, and DFT band structures. These are presented as plots and derived quantities in the main figures and Supplementary Information.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: The paper implies figure-specific data are embedded in the article and Supplementary Information, but it does not explicitly state that separate source-data files are deposited. The wording 'main data supporting the findings ... within the article and its Supplementary Information files' suggests figure data are provided at least in supplementary form, but the exact file structure is unknown.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned. DFT software and parameters are described, but not scripts or input files.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: Many figures are standard plots that can be reconstructed from the reported measurements and derived values, and the paper says supporting data are in the article and Supplementary Information. However, the exact raw file formats and whether complete source tables are provided are not specified.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper clearly distinguishes multiple measurement modalities and several derived analyses. Raw instrument data are implied, but the publication does not describe a formal public dataset, repository, or code release. Source-data availability is only indirectly implied by the statement that main data are in the article and Supplementary Information.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: Methods describe direct collection of STM, XRD, TEM, SQUID, XAS/XMCD, and ARPES measurements, indicating raw instrument data were generated.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The data availability statement says the main data supporting the findings are available within the article and Supplementary Information files, implying figure/source data are provided there.
classification_prior.both_evidence: The paper contains both direct measurement data and derived figure data, but does not specify a separate public raw-data repository.
data_availability_section_relevant: main data supporting the findings of this study are available within the article and its Supplementary Information files
Extra data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request
Methods: measurements were performed by STM, XRD, TEM, SQUID, XAS/XMCD, ARPES
DFT calculations were performed using VASP
classification_prior.review_notes: Be careful not to equate 'main data in article and Supplementary Information' with a fully open raw-data repository. The paper supports classification as having both raw measurements and processed/source figure data, but scripts are not provided.
